{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kajiyama Kofun | native_name =梶山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kajiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kajiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kajiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|27|5.39|N|134|17|41|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tottori (city)|Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|5.39|N|134|17|41|E}}}}}} [[file:梶山古墳彩色壁画.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Kajiyama Kofun mural]] The {{nihongo|'''Kajiyama Kofun'''|梶山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Okamasu, Kokufucho, neighborhood of the city of [[Tottori (city)|Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161988|title= 梶山古墳|trans-title= Kajiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kajiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==Overview== The Kokufucho area of Tottori city has numerous ancient burial mounds in the valleys of small hills which have been known for a long time; some were once used as air-raid shelters during [[World War II]]. The Kajiyama Kofun is located in horseshoe-shaped recess about 40 meters wide on the south side of a hill. The tumulus is 17 meters in diagonal length and has a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal shape|ja|八角墳}} (八角形墳) with sides of 2.5 to 8.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and in front of the entry a square platform two meters long and 14 meters wide for rituals is built over three levels with [[basalt]] stone walls.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] is made of cut [[tuff]]. It consists of a burial chamber itself, a gate, and an antechamber with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber measures 2.35 meters in length, 1.37 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. Both sides of the entrance gate are made of stone, and there is an indentation in the front for fitting a door stone. In the 1978 [[archaeological excavation|excavation]], a colored mural in red-yellow colors was discovered on the back wall of the burial chamber. On the upper part of the mural, are a triangular pattern and a concentric circle pattern symmetrically drawn in thin lines, and in the middle is a curved pattern of unknown design and a fish, 53-cm long, believed to have been a salmon or carp. Further down, there are two circles with a diameter of about 40 cm. Motifs such as triangular, circular, concentric circles, and fish are seen in decorated burial mounds in various parts of Japan, including the [[Kyushu]] region. Based on excavated artifacts such as [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] fragments, iron swords, metal fittings, thin gold-plates and the structure of the stone chamber, it is estimated to have been built between the latter half of the 6th century and the 7th century. The elaborate technique of cut stone in the stone chamber and the technique of the mural painting show a strong influence of continental culture. It is unknown who was buried in this tomb, but there is a theory that it was Prince Asagi, a member of the [[Imperial Family of Japan|imperial family]] who was exiled to [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] in 675. <ref name="Shiseki"/> Based on the discoveries made during the excavation, the tumulus has been reconstructed with a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal mound|ja|八角墳}} and square altar. The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In addition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />
The tumulus is located twenty-minute by car from [[Tottori Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].

The Kajiyama Kofun is located in horseshoe-shaped recess about 40 meters wide on the south side of a hill. The tumulus is 17 meters in diagonal length and has a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal shape|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} (八角形墳) with sides of 2.5 to 8.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and in front of the entry a square platform two meters long and 14 meters wide for rituals is built over three levels with [[basalt]] stone walls.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] is made of cut [[tuff]]. It consists of a burial chamber itself, a gate, and an antechamber with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber measures 2.35 meters in length, 1.37 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. Both sides of the entrance gate are made of stone, and there is an indentation in the front for fitting a door stone. In the 1978 [[archaeological excavation|excavation]], a colored mural in red-yellow colors was discovered on the back wall of the burial chamber. On the upper part of the mural, are a triangular pattern and a concentric circle pattern symmetrically drawn in thin lines, and in the middle is a curved pattern of unknown design and a fish, 53-cm long, believed to have been a salmon or carp. Further down, there are two circles with a diameter of about 40 cm. Motifs such as triangular, circular, concentric circles, and fish are seen in decorated burial mounds in various parts of Japan, including the [[Kyushu]] region. Based on excavated artifacts such as [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] fragments, iron swords, metal fittings, thin gold-plates and the structure of the stone chamber, it is estimated to have been built between the latter half of the 6th century and the 7th century. The elaborate technique of cut stone in the stone chamber and the technique of the mural painting show a strong influence of continental culture. It is unknown who was buried in this tomb, but there is a theory that it was Prince Asagi, a member of the [[Imperial Family of Japan|imperial family]] who was exiled to [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] in 675. <ref name="Shiseki"/> Based on the discoveries made during the excavation, the tumulus has been reconstructed with a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal mound|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} and square altar. The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In addition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kajiyama Kofun | native_name =梶山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kajiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kajiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kajiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|27|5.39|N|134|17|41|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tottori (city)|Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|5.39|N|134|17|41|E}}}}}} [[file:梶山古墳彩色壁画.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Kajiyama Kofun mural]] The {{nihongo|'''Kajiyama Kofun'''|梶山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Okamasu, Kokufucho, neighborhood of the city of [[Tottori (city)|Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161988|title= 梶山古墳|trans-title= Kajiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kajiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==Overview== The Kokufucho area of Tottori city has numerous ancient burial mounds in the valleys of small hills which have been known for a long time; some were once used as air-raid shelters during [[World War II]]. The Kajiyama Kofun is located in horseshoe-shaped recess about 40 meters wide on the south side of a hill. The tumulus is 17 meters in diagonal length and has a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal shape|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} (八角形墳) with sides of 2.5 to 8.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and in front of the entry a square platform two meters long and 14 meters wide for rituals is built over three levels with [[basalt]] stone walls.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] is made of cut [[tuff]]. It consists of a burial chamber itself, a gate, and an antechamber with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber measures 2.35 meters in length, 1.37 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. Both sides of the entrance gate are made of stone, and there is an indentation in the front for fitting a door stone. In the 1978 [[archaeological excavation|excavation]], a colored mural in red-yellow colors was discovered on the back wall of the burial chamber. On the upper part of the mural, are a triangular pattern and a concentric circle pattern symmetrically drawn in thin lines, and in the middle is a curved pattern of unknown design and a fish, 53-cm long, believed to have been a salmon or carp. Further down, there are two circles with a diameter of about 40 cm. Motifs such as triangular, circular, concentric circles, and fish are seen in decorated burial mounds in various parts of Japan, including the [[Kyushu]] region. Based on excavated artifacts such as [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] fragments, iron swords, metal fittings, thin gold-plates and the structure of the stone chamber, it is estimated to have been built between the latter half of the 6th century and the 7th century. The elaborate technique of cut stone in the stone chamber and the technique of the mural painting show a strong influence of continental culture. It is unknown who was buried in this tomb, but there is a theory that it was Prince Asagi, a member of the [[Imperial Family of Japan|imperial family]] who was exiled to [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] in 675. <ref name="Shiseki"/> Based on the discoveries made during the excavation, the tumulus has been reconstructed with a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal mound|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} and square altar. The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In addition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />
The tumulus is located twenty-minute by car from [[Tottori Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161988|title= 梶山古墳|trans-title= Kajiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kajiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==Overview== The Kokufucho area of Tottori city has numerous ancient burial mounds in the valleys of small hills which have been known for a long time; some were once used as air-raid shelters during [[World War II]]. The Kajiyama Kofun is located in horseshoe-shaped recess about 40 meters wide on the south side of a hill. The tumulus is 17 meters in diagonal length and has a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal shape|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} (八角形墳) with sides of 2.5 to 8.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and in front of the entry a square platform two meters long and 14 meters wide for rituals is built over three levels with [[basalt]] stone walls.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] is made of cut [[tuff]]. It consists of a burial chamber itself, a gate, and an antechamber with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber measures 2.35 meters in length, 1.37 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. Both sides of the entrance gate are made of stone, and there is an indentation in the front for fitting a door stone. In the 1978 [[archaeological excavation|excavation]], a colored mural in red-yellow colors was discovered on the back wall of the burial chamber. On the upper part of the mural, are a triangular pattern and a concentric circle pattern symmetrically drawn in thin lines, and in the middle is a curved pattern of unknown design and a fish, 53-cm long, believed to have been a salmon or carp. Further down, there are two circles with a diameter of about 40 cm. Motifs such as triangular, circular, concentric circles, and fish are seen in decorated burial mounds in various parts of Japan, including the [[Kyushu]] region. Based on excavated artifacts such as [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] fragments, iron swords, metal fittings, thin gold-plates and the structure of the stone chamber, it is estimated to have been built between the latter half of the 6th century and the 7th century. The elaborate technique of cut stone in the stone chamber and the technique of the mural painting show a strong influence of continental culture. It is unknown who was buried in this tomb, but there is a theory that it was Prince Asagi, a member of the [[Imperial Family of Japan|imperial family]] who was exiled to [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] in 675. <ref name="Shiseki"/> Based on the discoveries made during the excavation, the tumulus has been reconstructed with a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal mound|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} and square altar. The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In addition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />
The tumulus is located twenty-minute by car from [[Tottori Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161988|title= 梶山古墳|trans-title= Kajiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kajiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==Overview== The Kokufucho area of Tottori city has numerous ancient burial mounds in the valleys of small hills which have been known for a long time; some were once used as air-raid shelters during [[World War II]]. The Kajiyama Kofun is located in horseshoe-shaped recess about 40 meters wide on the south side of a hill. The tumulus is 17 meters in diagonal length and has a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal shape|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} (八角形墳) with sides of 2.5 to 8.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and in front of the entry a square platform two meters long and 14 meters wide for rituals is built over three levels with [[basalt]] stone walls.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] is made of cut [[tuff]]. It consists of a burial chamber itself, a gate, and an antechamber with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber measures 2.35 meters in length, 1.37 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. Both sides of the entrance gate are made of stone, and there is an indentation in the front for fitting a door stone. In the 1978 [[archaeological excavation|excavation]], a colored mural in red-yellow colors was discovered on the back wall of the burial chamber. On the upper part of the mural, are a triangular pattern and a concentric circle pattern symmetrically drawn in thin lines, and in the middle is a curved pattern of unknown design and a fish, 53-cm long, believed to have been a salmon or carp. Further down, there are two circles with a diameter of about 40 cm. Motifs such as triangular, circular, concentric circles, and fish are seen in decorated burial mounds in various parts of Japan, including the [[Kyushu]] region. Based on excavated artifacts such as [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] fragments, iron swords, metal fittings, thin gold-plates and the structure of the stone chamber, it is estimated to have been built between the latter half of the 6th century and the 7th century. The elaborate technique of cut stone in the stone chamber and the technique of the mural painting show a strong influence of continental culture. It is unknown who was buried in this tomb, but there is a theory that it was Prince Asagi, a member of the [[Imperial Family of Japan|imperial family]] who was exiled to [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] in 675. <ref name="Shiseki"/> Based on the discoveries made during the excavation, the tumulus has been reconstructed with a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal mound|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} and square altar. The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In addition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />
The tumulus is located twenty-minute by car from [[Tottori Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161988|title= 梶山古墳|trans-title= Kajiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kajiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==Overview== The Kokufucho area of Tottori city has numerous ancient burial mounds in the valleys of small hills which have been known for a long time; some were once used as air-raid shelters during [[World War II]]. The Kajiyama Kofun is located in horseshoe-shaped recess about 40 meters wide on the south side of a hill. The tumulus is 17 meters in diagonal length and has a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal shape|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} (八角形墳) with sides of 2.5 to 8.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and in front of the entry a square platform two meters long and 14 meters wide for rituals is built over three levels with [[basalt]] stone walls.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] is made of cut [[tuff]]. It consists of a burial chamber itself, a gate, and an antechamber with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber measures 2.35 meters in length, 1.37 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. Both sides of the entrance gate are made of stone, and there is an indentation in the front for fitting a door stone. In the 1978 [[archaeological excavation|excavation]], a colored mural in red-yellow colors was discovered on the back wall of the burial chamber. On the upper part of the mural, are a triangular pattern and a concentric circle pattern symmetrically drawn in thin lines, and in the middle is a curved pattern of unknown design and a fish, 53-cm long, believed to have been a salmon or carp. Further down, there are two circles with a diameter of about 40 cm. Motifs such as triangular, circular, concentric circles, and fish are seen in decorated burial mounds in various parts of Japan, including the [[Kyushu]] region. Based on excavated artifacts such as [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] fragments, iron swords, metal fittings, thin gold-plates and the structure of the stone chamber, it is estimated to have been built between the latter half of the 6th century and the 7th century. The elaborate technique of cut stone in the stone chamber and the technique of the mural painting show a strong influence of continental culture. It is unknown who was buried in this tomb, but there is a theory that it was Prince Asagi, a member of the [[Imperial Family of Japan|imperial family]] who was exiled to [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] in 675. <ref name="Shiseki"/> Based on the discoveries made during the excavation, the tumulus has been reconstructed with a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal mound|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} and square altar. The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In addition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />
The tumulus is located twenty-minute by car from [[Tottori Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]

[[Category:qq-Kajiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==Overview== The Kokufucho area of Tottori city has numerous ancient burial mounds in the valleys of small hills which have been known for a long time; some were once used as air-raid shelters during [[World War II]]. The Kajiyama Kofun is located in horseshoe-shaped recess about 40 meters wide on the south side of a hill. The tumulus is 17 meters in diagonal length and has a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal shape|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} (八角形墳) with sides of 2.5 to 8.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and in front of the entry a square platform two meters long and 14 meters wide for rituals is built over three levels with [[basalt]] stone walls.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] is made of cut [[tuff]]. It consists of a burial chamber itself, a gate, and an antechamber with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber measures 2.35 meters in length, 1.37 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. Both sides of the entrance gate are made of stone, and there is an indentation in the front for fitting a door stone. In the 1978 [[archaeological excavation|excavation]], a colored mural in red-yellow colors was discovered on the back wall of the burial chamber. On the upper part of the mural, are a triangular pattern and a concentric circle pattern symmetrically drawn in thin lines, and in the middle is a curved pattern of unknown design and a fish, 53-cm long, believed to have been a salmon or carp. Further down, there are two circles with a diameter of about 40 cm. Motifs such as triangular, circular, concentric circles, and fish are seen in decorated burial mounds in various parts of Japan, including the [[Kyushu]] region. Based on excavated artifacts such as [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] fragments, iron swords, metal fittings, thin gold-plates and the structure of the stone chamber, it is estimated to have been built between the latter half of the 6th century and the 7th century. The elaborate technique of cut stone in the stone chamber and the technique of the mural painting show a strong influence of continental culture. It is unknown who was buried in this tomb, but there is a theory that it was Prince Asagi, a member of the [[Imperial Family of Japan|imperial family]] who was exiled to [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] in 675. <ref name="Shiseki"/> Based on the discoveries made during the excavation, the tumulus has been reconstructed with a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal mound|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} and square altar. The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In addition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />
The tumulus is located twenty-minute by car from [[Tottori Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]

The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In addition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />
The tumulus is located twenty-minute by car from [[Tottori Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kajiyama Kofun | native_name =梶山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kajiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kajiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kajiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|27|5.39|N|134|17|41|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tottori (city)|Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|5.39|N|134|17|41|E}}}}}} [[file:梶山古墳彩色壁画.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Kajiyama Kofun mural]] The {{nihongo|'''Kajiyama Kofun'''|梶山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Okamasu, Kokufucho, neighborhood of the city of [[Tottori (city)|Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161988|title= 梶山古墳|trans-title= Kajiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Kajiyama Kofun-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==Overview== The Kokufucho area of Tottori city has numerous ancient burial mounds in the valleys of small hills which have been known for a long time; some were once used as air-raid shelters during [[World War II]]. The Kajiyama Kofun is located in horseshoe-shaped recess about 40 meters wide on the south side of a hill. The tumulus is 17 meters in diagonal length and has a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal shape|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} (八角形墳) with sides of 2.5 to 8.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and in front of the entry a square platform two meters long and 14 meters wide for rituals is built over three levels with [[basalt]] stone walls.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] is made of cut [[tuff]]. It consists of a burial chamber itself, a gate, and an antechamber with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber measures 2.35 meters in length, 1.37 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. Both sides of the entrance gate are made of stone, and there is an indentation in the front for fitting a door stone. In the 1978 [[archaeological excavation|excavation]], a colored mural in red-yellow colors was discovered on the back wall of the burial chamber. On the upper part of the mural, are a triangular pattern and a concentric circle pattern symmetrically drawn in thin lines, and in the middle is a curved pattern of unknown design and a fish, 53-cm long, believed to have been a salmon or carp. Further down, there are two circles with a diameter of about 40 cm. Motifs such as triangular, circular, concentric circles, and fish are seen in decorated burial mounds in various parts of Japan, including the [[Kyushu]] region. Based on excavated artifacts such as [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] fragments, iron swords, metal fittings, thin gold-plates and the structure of the stone chamber, it is estimated to have been built between the latter half of the 6th century and the 7th century. The elaborate technique of cut stone in the stone chamber and the technique of the mural painting show a strong influence of continental culture. It is unknown who was buried in this tomb, but there is a theory that it was Prince Asagi, a member of the [[Imperial Family of Japan|imperial family]] who was exiled to [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] in 675. <ref name="Shiseki"/> Based on the discoveries made during the excavation, the tumulus has been reconstructed with a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal mound|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} and square altar. The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In addition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />
The tumulus is located twenty-minute by car from [[Tottori Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kajiyama Kofun | native_name =梶山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kajiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kajiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kajiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|27|5.39|N|134|17|41|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tottori (city)|Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|5.39|N|134|17|41|E}}}}}} [[file:梶山古墳彩色壁画.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Kajiyama Kofun mural]] The {{nihongo|'''Kajiyama Kofun'''|梶山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Okamasu, Kokufucho, neighborhood of the city of [[Tottori (city)|Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161988|title= 梶山古墳|trans-title= Kajiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Kajiyama Kofun--> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==Overview== The Kokufucho area of Tottori city has numerous ancient burial mounds in the valleys of small hills which have been known for a long time; some were once used as air-raid shelters during [[World War II]]. The Kajiyama Kofun is located in horseshoe-shaped recess about 40 meters wide on the south side of a hill. The tumulus is 17 meters in diagonal length and has a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal shape|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} (八角形墳) with sides of 2.5 to 8.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and in front of the entry a square platform two meters long and 14 meters wide for rituals is built over three levels with [[basalt]] stone walls.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] is made of cut [[tuff]]. It consists of a burial chamber itself, a gate, and an antechamber with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber measures 2.35 meters in length, 1.37 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. Both sides of the entrance gate are made of stone, and there is an indentation in the front for fitting a door stone. In the 1978 [[archaeological excavation|excavation]], a colored mural in red-yellow colors was discovered on the back wall of the burial chamber. On the upper part of the mural, are a triangular pattern and a concentric circle pattern symmetrically drawn in thin lines, and in the middle is a curved pattern of unknown design and a fish, 53-cm long, believed to have been a salmon or carp. Further down, there are two circles with a diameter of about 40 cm. Motifs such as triangular, circular, concentric circles, and fish are seen in decorated burial mounds in various parts of Japan, including the [[Kyushu]] region. Based on excavated artifacts such as [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] fragments, iron swords, metal fittings, thin gold-plates and the structure of the stone chamber, it is estimated to have been built between the latter half of the 6th century and the 7th century. The elaborate technique of cut stone in the stone chamber and the technique of the mural painting show a strong influence of continental culture. It is unknown who was buried in this tomb, but there is a theory that it was Prince Asagi, a member of the [[Imperial Family of Japan|imperial family]] who was exiled to [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] in 675. <ref name="Shiseki"/> Based on the discoveries made during the excavation, the tumulus has been reconstructed with a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal mound|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} and square altar. The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In addition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />
The tumulus is located twenty-minute by car from [[Tottori Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]]Kajiyama Kofun[[en:Kajiyama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new qqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:八角墳]][[sn:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:八角墳]] [[Category:八角墳]][[sn:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:八角墳]] [[Category:八角墳]][[sn:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:八角墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kajiyama Kofun | native_name =梶山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kajiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kajiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kajiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|27|5.39|N|134|17|41|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tottori (city)|Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|5.39|N|134|17|41|E}}}}}} [[file:梶山古墳彩色壁画.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Kajiyama Kofun mural]] The {{nihongo|'''Kajiyama Kofun'''|梶山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Okamasu, Kokufucho, neighborhood of the city of [[Tottori (city)|Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161988|title= 梶山古墳|trans-title= Kajiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Kajiyama Kofun--> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==Overview== The Kokufucho area of Tottori city has numerous ancient burial mounds in the valleys of small hills which have been known for a long time; some were once used as air-raid shelters during [[World War II]]. The Kajiyama Kofun is located in horseshoe-shaped recess about 40 meters wide on the south side of a hill. The tumulus is 17 meters in diagonal length and has a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal shape|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} (八角形墳) with sides of 2.5 to 8.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and in front of the entry a square platform two meters long and 14 meters wide for rituals is built over three levels with [[basalt]] stone walls.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] is made of cut [[tuff]]. It consists of a burial chamber itself, a gate, and an antechamber with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber measures 2.35 meters in length, 1.37 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. Both sides of the entrance gate are made of stone, and there is an indentation in the front for fitting a door stone. In the 1978 [[archaeological excavation|excavation]], a colored mural in red-yellow colors was discovered on the back wall of the burial chamber. On the upper part of the mural, are a triangular pattern and a concentric circle pattern symmetrically drawn in thin lines, and in the middle is a curved pattern of unknown design and a fish, 53-cm long, believed to have been a salmon or carp. Further down, there are two circles with a diameter of about 40 cm. Motifs such as triangular, circular, concentric circles, and fish are seen in decorated burial mounds in various parts of Japan, including the [[Kyushu]] region. Based on excavated artifacts such as [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] fragments, iron swords, metal fittings, thin gold-plates and the structure of the stone chamber, it is estimated to have been built between the latter half of the 6th century and the 7th century. The elaborate technique of cut stone in the stone chamber and the technique of the mural painting show a strong influence of continental culture. It is unknown who was buried in this tomb, but there is a theory that it was Prince Asagi, a member of the [[Imperial Family of Japan|imperial family]] who was exiled to [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] in 675. <ref name="Shiseki"/> Based on the discoveries made during the excavation, the tumulus has been reconstructed with a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal mound|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} and square altar. The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In addition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />
The tumulus is located twenty-minute by car from [[Tottori Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161988|title= 梶山古墳|trans-title= Kajiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:U completion categories]]
[[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Kajiyama Kofun--> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==Overview== The Kokufucho area of Tottori city has numerous ancient burial mounds in the valleys of small hills which have been known for a long time; some were once used as air-raid shelters during [[World War II]]. The Kajiyama Kofun is located in horseshoe-shaped recess about 40 meters wide on the south side of a hill. The tumulus is 17 meters in diagonal length and has a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal shape|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} (八角形墳) with sides of 2.5 to 8.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and in front of the entry a square platform two meters long and 14 meters wide for rituals is built over three levels with [[basalt]] stone walls.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] is made of cut [[tuff]]. It consists of a burial chamber itself, a gate, and an antechamber with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber measures 2.35 meters in length, 1.37 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. Both sides of the entrance gate are made of stone, and there is an indentation in the front for fitting a door stone. In the 1978 [[archaeological excavation|excavation]], a colored mural in red-yellow colors was discovered on the back wall of the burial chamber. On the upper part of the mural, are a triangular pattern and a concentric circle pattern symmetrically drawn in thin lines, and in the middle is a curved pattern of unknown design and a fish, 53-cm long, believed to have been a salmon or carp. Further down, there are two circles with a diameter of about 40 cm. Motifs such as triangular, circular, concentric circles, and fish are seen in decorated burial mounds in various parts of Japan, including the [[Kyushu]] region. Based on excavated artifacts such as [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] fragments, iron swords, metal fittings, thin gold-plates and the structure of the stone chamber, it is estimated to have been built between the latter half of the 6th century and the 7th century. The elaborate technique of cut stone in the stone chamber and the technique of the mural painting show a strong influence of continental culture. It is unknown who was buried in this tomb, but there is a theory that it was Prince Asagi, a member of the [[Imperial Family of Japan|imperial family]] who was exiled to [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] in 675. <ref name="Shiseki"/> Based on the discoveries made during the excavation, the tumulus has been reconstructed with a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal mound|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} and square altar. The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In addition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />
The tumulus is located twenty-minute by car from [[Tottori Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161988|title= 梶山古墳|trans-title= Kajiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:U completion categories]]
<!--Kajiyama Kofun--> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==Overview== The Kokufucho area of Tottori city has numerous ancient burial mounds in the valleys of small hills which have been known for a long time; some were once used as air-raid shelters during [[World War II]]. The Kajiyama Kofun is located in horseshoe-shaped recess about 40 meters wide on the south side of a hill. The tumulus is 17 meters in diagonal length and has a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal shape|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} (八角形墳) with sides of 2.5 to 8.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and in front of the entry a square platform two meters long and 14 meters wide for rituals is built over three levels with [[basalt]] stone walls.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] is made of cut [[tuff]]. It consists of a burial chamber itself, a gate, and an antechamber with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber measures 2.35 meters in length, 1.37 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. Both sides of the entrance gate are made of stone, and there is an indentation in the front for fitting a door stone. In the 1978 [[archaeological excavation|excavation]], a colored mural in red-yellow colors was discovered on the back wall of the burial chamber. On the upper part of the mural, are a triangular pattern and a concentric circle pattern symmetrically drawn in thin lines, and in the middle is a curved pattern of unknown design and a fish, 53-cm long, believed to have been a salmon or carp. Further down, there are two circles with a diameter of about 40 cm. Motifs such as triangular, circular, concentric circles, and fish are seen in decorated burial mounds in various parts of Japan, including the [[Kyushu]] region. Based on excavated artifacts such as [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] fragments, iron swords, metal fittings, thin gold-plates and the structure of the stone chamber, it is estimated to have been built between the latter half of the 6th century and the 7th century. The elaborate technique of cut stone in the stone chamber and the technique of the mural painting show a strong influence of continental culture. It is unknown who was buried in this tomb, but there is a theory that it was Prince Asagi, a member of the [[Imperial Family of Japan|imperial family]] who was exiled to [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] in 675. <ref name="Shiseki"/> Based on the discoveries made during the excavation, the tumulus has been reconstructed with a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal mound|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} and square altar. The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In addition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />
The tumulus is located twenty-minute by car from [[Tottori Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]

The Kajiyama Kofun is located in horseshoe-shaped recess about 40 meters wide on the south side of a hill. The tumulus is 17 meters in diagonal length and has a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal shape|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} (八角形墳) with sides of 2.5 to 8.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and in front of the entry a square platform two meters long and 14 meters wide for rituals is built over three levels with [[basalt]] stone walls.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] is made of cut [[tuff]]. It consists of a burial chamber itself, a gate, and an antechamber with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber measures 2.35 meters in length, 1.37 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. Both sides of the entrance gate are made of stone, and there is an indentation in the front for fitting a door stone. In the 1978 [[archaeological excavation|excavation]], a colored mural in red-yellow colors was discovered on the back wall of the burial chamber. On the upper part of the mural, are a triangular pattern and a concentric circle pattern symmetrically drawn in thin lines, and in the miqqle is a curved pattern of unknown design and a fish, 53-cm long, believed to have been a salmon or carp. Further down, there are two circles with a diameter of about 40 cm. Motifs such as triangular, circular, concentric circles, and fish are seen in decorated burial mounds in various parts of Japan, including the [[Kyushu]] region. Based on excavated artifacts such as [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] fragments, iron swords, metal fittings, thin gold-plates and the structure of the stone chamber, it is estimated to have been built between the latter half of the 6th century and the 7th century. The elaborate technique of cut stone in the stone chamber and the technique of the mural painting show a strong influence of continental culture. It is unknown who was buried in this tomb, but there is a theory that it was Prince Asagi, a member of the [[Imperial Family of Japan|imperial family]] who was exiled to [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] in 675. <ref name="Shiseki"/> Based on the discoveries made during the excavation, the tumulus has been reconstructed with a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal mound|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} and square altar. The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In aqqition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />
The tumulus is located twenty-minute by car from [[Tottori Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kajiyama Kofun | native_name =梶山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kajiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kajiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kajiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|27|5.39|N|134|17|41|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tottori (city)|Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|5.39|N|134|17|41|E}}}}}} [[file:梶山古墳彩色壁画.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Kajiyama Kofun mural]] The {{nihongo|'''Kajiyama Kofun'''|梶山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Okamasu, Kokufucho, neighborhood of the city of [[Tottori (city)|Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The Kajiyama Kofun is located in horseshoe-shaped recess about 40 meters wide on the south side of a hill. It consists of a burial chamber itself, a gate, and an antechamber with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber measures 2.35 meters in length, 1.37 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. Both sides of the entrance gate are made of stone, and there is an indentation in the front for fitting a door stone. In the 1978 [[archaeological excavation|excavation]], a colored mural in red-yellow colors was discovered on the back wall of the burial chamber. On the upper part of the mural, are a triangular pattern and a concentric circle pattern symmetrically drawn in thin lines, and in the miqqle is a curved pattern of unknown design and a fish, 53-cm long, believed to have been a salmon or carp. Further down, there are two circles with a diameter of about 40 cm. Motifs such as triangular, circular, concentric circles, and fish are seen in decorated burial mounds in various parts of Japan, including the [[Kyushu]] region. Based on excavated artifacts such as [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] fragments, iron swords, metal fittings, thin gold-plates and the structure of the stone chamber, it is estimated to have been built between the latter half of the 6th century and the 7th century. The elaborate technique of cut stone in the stone chamber and the technique of the mural painting show a strong influence of continental culture. The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In aqqition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />
The tumulus is located twenty-minute by car from [[Tottori Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]]Kajiyama Kofun[[en:Kajiyama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new qqqqq]] [[Category:八角墳]][[sn:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:八角墳]] [[Category:八角墳]][[sn:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:八角墳]] [[Category:八角墳]][[sn:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:八角墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kajiyama Kofun | native_name =梶山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kajiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kajiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kajiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|27|5.39|N|134|17|41|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tottori (city)|Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|5.39|N|134|17|41|E}}}}}} [[file:梶山古墳彩色壁画.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Kajiyama Kofun mural]] The {{nihongo|'''Kajiyama Kofun'''|梶山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Okamasu, Kokufucho, neighborhood of the city of [[Tottori (city)|Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161988|title= 梶山古墳|trans-title= Kajiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:U completion categories]]
<!--Kajiyama Kofun--> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==Overview== The Kokufucho area of Tottori city has numerous ancient burial mounds in the valleys of small hills which have been known for a long time; some were once used as air-raid shelters during [[World War II]]. The Kajiyama Kofun is located in horseshoe-shaped recess about 40 meters wide on the south side of a hill. The tumulus is 17 meters in diagonal length and has a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal shape|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} (八角形墳) with sides of 2.5 to 8.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and in front of the entry a square platform two meters long and 14 meters wide for rituals is built over three levels with [[basalt]] stone walls.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] is made of cut [[tuff]]. It consists of a burial chamber itself, a gate, and an antechamber with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber measures 2.35 meters in length, 1.37 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. Both sides of the entrance gate are made of stone, and there is an indentation in the front for fitting a door stone. In the 1978 [[archaeological excavation|excavation]], a colored mural in red-yellow colors was discovered on the back wall of the burial chamber. On the upper part of the mural, are a triangular pattern and a concentric circle pattern symmetrically drawn in thin lines, and in the miqqle is a curved pattern of unknown design and a fish, 53-cm long, believed to have been a salmon or carp. Further down, there are two circles with a diameter of about 40 cm. Motifs such as triangular, circular, concentric circles, and fish are seen in decorated burial mounds in various parts of Japan, including the [[Kyushu]] region. Based on excavated artifacts such as [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] fragments, iron swords, metal fittings, thin gold-plates and the structure of the stone chamber, it is estimated to have been built between the latter half of the 6th century and the 7th century. The elaborate technique of cut stone in the stone chamber and the technique of the mural painting show a strong influence of continental culture. It is unknown who was buried in this tomb, but there is a theory that it was Prince Asagi, a member of the [[Imperial Family of Japan|imperial family]] who was exiled to [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] in 675. <ref name="Shiseki"/> Based on the discoveries made during the excavation, the tumulus has been reconstructed with a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal mound|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} and square altar. The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In aqqition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />
The tumulus is located twenty-minute by car from [[Tottori Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]]Kajiyama Kofun[[en:Kajiyama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new qqqq]] [[Category:八角墳]][[sn:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:八角墳]] [[Category:八角墳]][[sn:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:八角墳]] [[Category:八角墳]][[sn:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:八角墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kajiyama Kofun | native_name =梶山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kajiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kajiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kajiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|27|5.39|N|134|17|41|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tottori (city)|Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|5.39|N|134|17|41|E}}}}}} [[file:梶山古墳彩色壁画.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Kajiyama Kofun mural]] The {{nihongo|'''Kajiyama Kofun'''|梶山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Okamasu, Kokufucho, neighborhood of the city of [[Tottori (city)|Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161988|title= 梶山古墳|trans-title= Kajiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:U completion categories]]
<!--Kajiyama Kofun--> [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kajiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==Overview== The Kokufucho area of Tottori city has numerous ancient burial mounds in the valleys of small hills which have been known for a long time; some were once used as air-raid shelters during [[World War II]]. The Kajiyama Kofun is located in horseshoe-shaped recess about 40 meters wide on the south side of a hill. The tumulus is 17 meters in diagonal length and has a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal shape|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} (八角形墳) with sides of 2.5 to 8.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and in front of the entry a square platform two meters long and 14 meters wide for rituals is built over three levels with [[basalt]] stone walls.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] is made of cut [[tuff]]. It consists of a burial chamber itself, a gate, and an antechamber with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber measures 2.35 meters in length, 1.37 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. Both sides of the entrance gate are made of stone, and there is an indentation in the front for fitting a door stone. In the 1978 [[archaeological excavation|excavation]], a colored mural in red-yellow colors was discovered on the back wall of the burial chamber. On the upper part of the mural, are a triangular pattern and a concentric circle pattern symmetrically drawn in thin lines, and in the miqqle is a curved pattern of unknown design and a fish, 53-cm long, believed to have been a salmon or carp. Further down, there are two circles with a diameter of about 40 cm. Motifs such as triangular, circular, concentric circles, and fish are seen in decorated burial mounds in various parts of Japan, including the [[Kyushu]] region. Based on excavated artifacts such as [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] fragments, iron swords, metal fittings, thin gold-plates and the structure of the stone chamber, it is estimated to have been built between the latter half of the 6th century and the 7th century. The elaborate technique of cut stone in the stone chamber and the technique of the mural painting show a strong influence of continental culture. It is unknown who was buried in this tomb, but there is a theory that it was Prince Asagi, a member of the [[Imperial Family of Japan|imperial family]] who was exiled to [[Inaba Province|Inaba]] in 675. <ref name="Shiseki"/> Based on the discoveries made during the excavation, the tumulus has been reconstructed with a modified {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=octagonal mound|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} and square altar. The burial chamber, formerly open and used by children as a playground, now has a door to preserve the wall paintings. It is now open to the public for about three days in spring or autumn when the temperature difference between the outside and the stone chamber is small. In aqqition, a full-size replica of the burial chamber is displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Inaba Manyo Historical Museum'''|因幡万葉歴史館||}}.<ref name="Shiseki" />
The tumulus is located twenty-minute by car from [[Tottori Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
